content_markdown: The power per unit length $P$ of an oscillating water column (OWC) is due to the action of a surface wave of amplitude $A$. Which of the following correctly relates $P$ and $A$, and correctly identifies the nature of the energy of the water column?

diagram_metadata.description: A table with two columns: 'Relation between P and A' and 'Nature of energy'.

vibe_explanation: In any simple harmonic motion or wave system, the energy (and thus the power) is proportional to the square of the amplitude ($P \propto A^2$). Furthermore, mechanical waves like water waves involve both the movement of particles (kinetic energy) and the displacement against gravity or pressure (potential energy).
